design_philosophy.core_loop: Select vehicle (helicopter or jeep), navigate vertically scrolling stages, destroy enemies and bosses, collect power-ups (shields, weapon upgrades), progress through levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Sustained bullet avoidance and resource management while maintaining offensive output; dual-vehicle asymmetry creates distinct playstyle tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting specialized military vehicle (helicopter/jeep) in armed combat against overwhelming enemy forces; mastery of vehicle-specific mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Forces permanent vehicle choice at game start (helicopter OR jeep), eliminating hybrid playstyle; dual-player mode mandates both vehicles simultaneously, creating interdependent unit tactics. Boss destruction directly funds weapon progression, making combat encounters essential upgrade gates rather than optional challenges

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Vehicle selection at game start provides strategic difficulty differentiation (helicopter vs jeep handling/firepower profiles); dual-player cooperative mode alters enemy density and tactical requirements

progression.upgrade_system: Boss destruction grants forward-firing gun upgrades; shield power-ups provide temporary invincibility when picked up or can be detonated for screen-clear effect
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss defeats unlock incremental weapon improvements; power-up drops contingent on enemy destruction
replayability_hooks: Alternate vehicle selection (helicopter vs jeep) changes mechanical feel and difficulty curve
Two-player cooperative mode creates asymmetric tactical requirements versus single-player
Shield power-up detonation strategy (pickup for invincibility vs detonate for screen clear) creates moment-to-moment decision trees

world_building_techniques: Acronym worldbuilding: SWIV = 'Special Weapons Interdiction Vehicle' (manual-only exposition)
Military vehicle aesthetic establishes setting implicitly through hardware design
Enemy unit types suggest faction/threat hierarchy through visual design

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters function as mandatory progression gates and weapon upgrade sources; periodic appearance pattern structures level pacing

faction_indicators: Enemy units suggest organized military opposition (homing missile designation 'SWIV' indicates adversary tech parity)
Dual-vehicle protagonist design implies task-specialized military operations (air/ground coordination)
danger_signals: Boss periodicity creates escalating threat rhythm
Weapon upgrade gating behind boss destruction means difficulty inflection points coincide with resource availability
environmental_storytelling: Vertical scrolling perspective establishes forward-momentum military operation (invasion/defensive push)
Enemy spawning patterns suggest defensive positions or advancing enemy formations

sources_used: wikipedia - SWIV article (gameplay mechanics, vehicle selection, power-up systems, boss structure, multiplayer design, development heritage from Silkworm)
